reviews.review_description: Love this puzzle game and the kookie graphics. Thx

I had this in my library a long time and never played til today. wow is it awesome great use of vr, visuals, gameplay and fantastic clever puzzles. I like how each level is very different and has completely different way for solution. I just did like 6 in a row and cant wait for me.so much detail and experimentation in each level a must have in anyone's library

Not many games make me gasp with delight and actually "ooh" and "ahh" when starting up. Gnog is such a game. I have no idea what I'm doing, but the environments and music are so gorgeous I don't really care. Slightly similar to VVR but better, not as frustrating and, as I said, a sensory feast.

If you want to increase the resolution of this game check here
https://youtu.be/rcusZ2nH0y4
or search "Open PC Reviews" channel on YouTube and look for "How to increase the resolution on your Oculus Go".

reviews.review_title: A trippy pop up book-like puzzle
reviews.review_description:
Great to see a game that just released on Steam and other platforms gets a release on Oculus Go also (it was already on PS4). 

Feels like a fresh new experience. Graphics are very jaggy, but overall it's smooth and good fun. Plays like a trippy pop up book where you can interact with a ton of things.

Although I have only played finished the first box, I thought it was worth it to share my experience. Double Fine Games (via KO_OP) deliver quality as usual.

Despite having some control issues and uneven difficulty, I think GNOG is a real gem. It has a terrific art style, great sound design, and rewarding game play. Each box contains a unique, self-contained world waiting to be unlocked. Though I have played similar puzzle games on other platforms, none of them were nearly as charming.  Though the game is pretty short, it is a very memorable experience. Its trippy visuals and unique world design help it to stand out in a market over-saturated by puzzle games.  

Read more of my thoughts here: http://www.oculusgogogo.com/reviews/games/gnog/
The best! I was influenced very much by design.

There is a lot to like in GNOG, and a bit that I don't. 

While it is colorful and fun (and has some nice music and sounds), I found the game a bit short and too easy. Younger players might appreciate it better than older ones, then. The game also crashed twice, both times near the end: once when I completed the last house in HOM-3, and right near the end of the credits. 

For its small price (and I bought it during a promotion), it gives you some good & relaxing fun.
Wonderful graphics and fun to play. Unfortunately I’m stuck on one game and can’t seem to move forward.

This must be one of the most wonderful surprises on Go. I kind of knew what to expect from the game, but screenshots and videos don't do the game justice. You must experience it in VR. It's trippy, cute and just a joy to play. Puzzles are not too difficult but not too easy either. Just right. Graphics are great as well. Smooth and crisp. A wonderful addition to Go library.

Very cool, definitely one one of the better games on the platform. I agree with the other reviewers about the resolution being a problem, it definitely needs some anti-aliasing or something. So almost perfect.
Very nice designed with great Soundtrack. Beautiful.
Love Gnog so much fun.

It's a fun little game where you have to play around with different "boxes" and figure out what it's all about. I just wish the graphics were more crisp. It's probably not using the full resolution of the Oculus Go so every line is jagged which is a shame since the game concept is beautiful. I hope they are going to fix the poor visuals so it will be a better experience.

UPDATE: I didn't receive an official response from the developer but I think they upgraded the graphics because it looks much better now. Thank you! Great little game!
I'm using a s10+, the game will not highlight any option on the title screen. Can't start the game.
Definently!

Purchased this game for my daughter who has hand arm mobility issues. While the graphics were beautiful, and it looked like it would be a great game, no matter how hard she tried, she was unable to make a lot of the movements required in this game. I would have given it five stars for the beautiful graphics, but I took it down to two stars, because they refused to refund even when I explained the circumstances. Do better!

reviews.review_title: GNOG is a must have game.
reviews.review_description: OK it is mainly pulling, pushing and twiddling and can be a bit awkward on the Go, but it is beautifully designed and so much fun that any small faults are forgive.

Considering the sad landscape here on the Oculus Go platform, this game is a real standout. The fact that it's not promoted more (never saw it in the Spotlight or Apps We Love, or, in fact, in any other section on the Oculus store home page) is criminal. It's one of those rare truly original multiplatform games that are also available on the Go. You can get it for the flat screen PC, PS4 and even iphone. And imagine pretty much any other Go/Gear-VR title on Steam - it would be demolished for being primitive, short and/or ugly. But Gnog has very positive reviews even though it costs more there. That should be all you need to know.

With all that said, this is a puzzle game and nothing more. There are no-to-very little VR thrills to be had here (although it's in full 3D VR, of course). There are also only 9 levels, but the game feels actually long enough, possibly due to pretty much zero repetition. Every puzzle is not like any other. Difficulty is just right - keep your eyes open and you won't need any hints. On the negative side, controls are a bit finicky and there have been no updates since August 2018, but it is to be expected considering they probably made zero profit on this particular version.

This is a great game. Very clever, creative and artistic. My only complaint is that there aren’t more levels as I would gladly pay for new content! The game is well polished and it’s clear the team spent time in crafting it.
